title: Dorosii

{{Quote|Dorosii the Hutt, Appointed Intermediate to the Senate, has assured Republic officials that they can resolve the cult violence without government intervention. Nonetheless, the Jedi Council is reportedly examining the situation and is likely to dispatch a taskforce.|HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|<ref name="hnn" />}}
'''Dorosii''' the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Senator/Legends|served]] as the [[Appointed Intermediate]] of the [[Periphery/Legends|Periphery]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Great Peace of the Republic|period]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Cult of M'dweshuu]]—a [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] [[Death/Legends|death]]-worshiping cult—launched a string of [[Cult of M'dweshuu Sisar Run murders|murders]] along the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]], a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] in the Periphery. Dorosii tried to reassure the Republic that Hutt authorities would end the cult's brutality without Republic interference; however, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] reportedly launched an investigation into the situation.

==Biography==
[[File:ThePeriphery.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Dorosii represented the Periphery (pictured on map) as an Appointed Intermediate to the Galactic Senate.]]
Dorosii the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Senator/Legends|represented]] the [[Periphery/Legends|Periphery]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|217|Dorosii the Hutt}}</ref>—a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] that bordered [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]<ref name="atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>—as an [[Appointed Intermediate]] to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Great Peace of the Republic|reign]].<ref name="hnn" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|regional/13228_1.html|Nikto Cultists Plague Sisar Run}} is set on the date [[Great ReSynchronization|13:2:28]], which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] according to the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> the [[Cult of M'dweshuu]]—a [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] [[Death/Legends|death]]-worshiping cult from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kintan/Legends|Kintan]]—launched a string of [[Cult of M'dweshuu Sisar Run murders|scattered murders]] along the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] in the Periphery and killed at least twenty-seven [[Sentience/Legends|beings]].<ref name="hnn" />

Following the outbreak of occult violence, Dorosii attempted to convince Republic officials that intervention in the situation was unnecessary, as local Hutt authorities would deal with the M'dweshuu attacks. Despite the Hutt's reassurances, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] planned to investigate the murders anyway and, according to [[HoloNet News]], likely intended to send a [[Task force/Legends|task force]] into the Periphery themselves. An edition of [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition]] later documented on both the M'dweshuu cult murders and Dorosii's exchanges with Republic officials.<ref name="hnn" />

==Personality and traits==
Dorosii believed that it was not necessary for the Republic to get involved in ending the M'dweshuu murders, though the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] did not share these views.<ref name="hnn" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dorosii was first mentioned in "Nikto Cultists Plague Sisar Run," an article<ref name="hnn" /> written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]<ref name="credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> and published in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 45|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #45]] on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]]<ref name="hnn" /> on [[February 28]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN45 date">Per {{JCFCite|url=posts/6042498|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=IAmTheDarkSide|date=2002-03-14|time=5:40 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20220914072556/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-4#post-6042498}}, [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 47|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #47]] was released on [[March 14]], [[2002]]. As [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] segments were published weekly, and {{HoloNetNewsWeb|45||A Closer Look}} was published two issues prior, it must have been released on [[February 28]] of the same year.</ref>
